title: Modern C#
summary: By the end, your developers write C# that's shorter, clearer and safer by using the language's recent improvements. More readability, fewer bugs.
outline: Advanced pattern matching and switch expressions
prerequisites: Basic C#, ability to read object-oriented code.

title: ASP.NET Core 10, fundamentals
summary: By the end, your team can design, configure and run a production-ready web application with ASP.NET Core 10.

title: ASP.NET Core 10, advanced
summary: By the end, your teams take a web app to production with confidence: secure access, performance, monitoring and reliable tests. Robust, not just working.
prerequisites: ASP.NET Core fundamentals or equivalent experience.

title: Blazor 10, Server, WASM and Hybrid
summary: By the end, your team builds modern, responsive web interfaces entirely in C#, without juggling several languages, and ships faster.
outline: Blazor Server vs WASM, tech choice

title: Entity Framework Core 10
summary: By the end, your developers handle your data reliably and fast: model, evolve and query a database with no nasty surprises.

title: Automated testing in .NET
summary: By the end, your team ships with peace of mind: useful automated tests that catch regressions early and let you deploy without holding your breath.

title: Azure for developers (AZ-204 prep)
summary: Prepare and pass the Azure AZ-204 certification, and leave able to build real cloud applications. The skills and the recognized credential.
outline: Key Vault, Managed Identity, App Configuration

title: Azure DevOps & CI/CD (AZ-400 prep)
summary: By the end, your teams automate software delivery and prepare the Azure AZ-400 certification: deploy more often, faster, with fewer errors.
prerequisites: Team project experience, basic Git, basic cloud.

title: Azure Functions & event-driven
summary: By the end, your team builds cloud apps that react in real time and scale on their own, with no servers to manage.
prerequisites: ASP.NET Core or Azure 204, async basics.

title: Azure App Service & containers
summary: By the end, your teams get your apps live on Azure reliably: zero-downtime updates and automatic scaling.
outline: Azure Container Registry and image signing

title: Azure security for developers
summary: By the end, your teams secure your Azure applications end to end and protect your sensitive data, without slowing delivery.
outline: Microsoft Entra ID and Conditional Access

title: Claude Code for teams
summary: By the end, your team rolls out Claude Code at scale and turns it into real productivity, with the guardrails that protect quality.

title: GitHub Copilot, advanced
summary: By the end, your developers get far more from GitHub Copilot than autocomplete to speed up their daily work, fully compliant.
prerequisites: Hands-on Copilot or other IDE assistant.

title: Designing your MCP servers
summary: By the end, your teams connect your AI tools to your own data and applications securely, through custom bridges (MCP servers) that keep your data under control.
prerequisites: Hands-on backend language (TS, Python or C#), API basics.

title: On-premise AI in the enterprise
summary: By the end, your teams run generative AI on your own servers, without sending your data outside. Ideal for privacy-sensitive sectors.
outline: Open-weight model landscape (Llama, Qwen, Mistral, Gemma)
Internal use cases, code, search, summarization

title: Clean Architecture in .NET
summary: By the end, your teams structure applications that stay easy to evolve and test over time. Less debt, more peace of mind.

title: Event-driven architecture & practical CQRS
summary: By the end, your teams design applications that stay smooth and reliable even under heavy load, applying the right patterns in the right place, no dogma.
prerequisites: ASP.NET Core, EF Core, message broker basics.

title: Modern observability, OpenTelemetry
summary: By the end, your teams know exactly what's happening in your apps in production and diagnose an incident in minutes rather than hours.

title: .NET performance & scalability
summary: By the end, your teams make your .NET applications noticeably faster and able to handle load. Measure first, optimize second, with gains to show.

title: CI/CD with GitHub Actions
summary: By the end, your teams automate testing and releases with GitHub Actions: ship more often, with confidence, no manual steps.
prerequisites: Git basics, hands-on with any CI.
